zotero.abstractNote: Multi-task reinforcement learning aims to improve the learning efficiency of an agent by leveraging knowledge across multiple tasks. A common approach is to share parameters among tasks, but this often leads to negative transfer when tasks are not sufficiently related. In this paper, we propose a novel approach to multi-task deep reinforcement learning that learns to share knowledge by dynamically selecting which parameters to share. We introduce a gating mechanism that learns to mask the weights of the neural network, allowing the agent to selectively share information across tasks. We evaluate our approach on a variety of multi-task reinforcement learning benchmarks and show that it outperforms existing methods in terms of both learning speed and final performance.
